explanation.rule: Advanced word formation often combines multiple processes: a prefix plus a suffix, or compounding two roots together.

groups.note: Creates adjectives meaning 'not possible to do'

examples.definition: to use something in the wrong way
to do something at the wrong time

groups.note: Two words combined to form a new meaning

wordForms.example: The judge declared the terms of the agreement unalterable.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to make something different from what it was

wordForms.example: The evidence against him was unarguable.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to give reasons for or against something

wordForms.example: The region had become ungovernable after years of conflict.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to control and direct a country or organisation

wordForms.definition: to use something in the wrong or inappropriate way
wordForms.example: The law was clearly being misapplied in this case.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to use for a particular purpose

wordForms.definition: to govern a country or organisation badly
wordForms.example: The country had been misgoverned for decades.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to control and direct a country or organisation

wordForms.definition: to do something at the wrong moment
wordForms.example: He mistimed his tackle and received a yellow card.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a point measured in hours, minutes, or seconds

wordForms.definition: to form a wrong opinion about someone or something
wordForms.example: She misjudged the distance and fell short of the target.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to form an opinion about something

wordForms.definition: information based on what people say rather than direct evidence
wordForms.example: Much of the report was based on hearsay rather than solid facts.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to perceive sounds with the ears

wordForms.example: The magazine has a reputation for highbrow cultural commentary.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a point far above the ground; elevated

wordForms.example: He was described as an upstanding member of the community.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to be in an upright position

wordForms.definition: too unpleasant or painful to endure
wordForms.example: The heat in the room was unbearable by midday.
Her matchless talent placed her in a class of her own.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to be equal to or the same as something else

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The terms of the deal were declared ___ by the court.
The logic of his argument was ___; nobody could disagree.
He ___ the law when dealing with the new employees.
The ___ nature of the story made it impossible to verify.
She ___ her jump and landed awkwardly on the mat.
The region had become ___ after decades of unrest.

transformExercises.sentence: The contract contained several ___ clauses that could not be renegotiated. (ALTER)
The verdict was based on ___ and had no solid evidence to support it. (HEAR)
The government had clearly ___ the situation for many years. (GOVERN)
The pain from the injury was ___ at times. (BEAR)
The publication was considered ___ by most readers. (HIGH)
She was ___ about the risks and was caught completely off guard. (JUDGE)
